data.title: brainstorm/preplanning: operational atomicity gaps not surfaced as acceptance criteria -> design gaps reach implementation
data.description:
## Incident Overview
Epic 36a7-dbb5 (plugin release channels) produced release.sh with a design gap: the script creates a git tag and bumps plugin.json but does not atomically update marketplace.json's dso source.ref. This gap was not flagged during brainstorm or preplanning — it was discovered only by the standard-tier code reviewer post-implementation as a 'minor' finding (non-blocking). A separate manual cutover commit was required, creating a desync window between the tag and the config. Bug 4beb-b2f1 tracks the fix to release.sh itself.

## The Process Failure
Brainstorm and preplanning evaluated the release workflow at the feature level (does a release script exist with safety gates?) but did not evaluate at the operational coordination level (must all artifacts that change together be updated in the same atomic operation?). The gap pattern is: 'Script A creates/updates artifact X, but configuration file Y that references X is not updated in the same step.'

## Expected Behavior
By the time a story exits preplanning with done definitions, any requirement that two artifacts must be updated together (atomically, in the same commit or script run) should appear explicitly as an acceptance criterion — not discovered post-implementation during code review.

## Analogous gaps this pattern would catch
- A migration script that adds a table but doesn't update a schema registry file
- A deployment script that bumps a version but doesn't update a manifest that references it
- An installer that adds a plugin entry but doesn't update an index file that must stay in sync

## Fix Direction
Add an 'operational atomicity' check to the brainstorm scrutiny pipeline and/or the preplanning AC generation step:
- For any story involving a script that creates or modifies a versioned artifact (tag, binary, package), ask: 'Is there another file that references this artifact that must be updated in the same operation?'
- If yes, require an explicit done definition: 'When this story is complete, <file> is updated atomically in the same script run / commit as <artifact>.'
- The red-team reviewer (dso:red-team-reviewer) is a natural insertion point for this check pattern.
